reason: config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override bound directly; resolveArtifactRoot node command intentionally skipped

findingDetails.title: Memo edge-count cache can miss dependency_edges written outside this store instance
findingDetails.claim: MemoStore snapshots dependency_edges count at construction and only updates it through its own insert path, so externally inserted edges can leave traversal and cycle checks incorrectly skipped.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either document single-writer ownership for dependency_edges or refresh the count before using the zero-row fast path when shared DB mutation is possible.

noveltyJustification: First correctness pass found one low-severity edge case in the zero-row fast path cache contract.
ruledOut: Weighted BFS seed exclusion, hop cap, and directed reachability termination were checked against implementation and equivalence tests without a blocker.

noveltyJustification: No new security findings; SQL reads use placeholders and normalized numeric IDs, and traversal helpers do not introduce new trust-boundary writes.
ruledOut: SQL injection through BFS traversal inputs was ruled out by placeholder binding in graph readers and numeric ID normalization before causal-boost traversal.

findingDetails.title: SC-002 requires p95 latency, but verification records only mean latency
findingDetails.claim: The packet marks SC-002 shipped even though the implemented benchmark helper computes a simple mean and the summary table reports mean, not p95 latency.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked tasks.md T008, implementation-summary latency table, and the benchmark helper/test body for percentile computation or recorded p95 output; none was present.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: REQ-003 only asks for a recorded benchmark, but SC-002 explicitly names p95, so the stricter success criterion remains unproven.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either update the benchmark to compute and record p95, or amend SC-002 to the measured mean metric before treating the criterion as complete.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after a p95 benchmark row is recorded or SC-002 is amended to match the mean-latency evidence.

results.summary: BFS equivalence and mean benchmark evidence exist, but the p95 success criterion is not proven.
Checked task claims carry concrete file or command evidence.

noveltyJustification: Traceability cross-check found one requirement evidence mismatch between SC-002 and the recorded latency metric.
ruledOut: Task evidence for code cutover and CTE-removal claim resolves to implementation files and source scan statement.

claim: The packet marks SC-002 shipped even though the implemented benchmark helper computes a simple mean and the summary table reports mean, not p95 latency.
counterevidenceSought: Checked tasks.md T008, implementation-summary latency table, and the benchmark helper/test body for percentile computation or recorded p95 output; none was present.
alternativeExplanation: REQ-003 only asks for a recorded benchmark, but SC-002 explicitly names p95, so the stricter success criterion remains unproven.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after a p95 benchmark row is recorded or SC-002 is amended to match the mean-latency evidence.

findingDetails.title: Latency benchmark is enforced as a deterministic unit-test assertion
findingDetails.claim: The equivalence suite asserts bfsMs <= cteMs from a 25-iteration in-process mean, so ordinary scheduler or cache noise can fail the release gate even when behavior is correct.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the benchmark helper and test body for warmup, percentile sampling, tolerance, or an advisory-only recording mode; none was present.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The local run recorded BFS faster than CTE, but a single local mean does not make the assertion stable across CI hosts and concurrent load.
findingDetails.recommendation: Keep the equivalence assertions hard, but make the performance comparison advisory or use a warmed percentile benchmark with tolerance outside the normal unit test path.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 after the latency comparison is moved to advisory reporting or hardened with warmup, percentile sampling, and a documented tolerance.

results.summary: Performance proof exists but remains metric-mismatched and fragile as a hard unit-test gate.
Task rows include evidence; the concern is the strength and stability of the latency gate, not missing task evidence.

noveltyJustification: Maintainability pass found one release-gate stability issue around the benchmark assertion.
ruledOut: No additional maintainability blocker found in the shared traversal abstraction or storage port adapter.

results.summary: Stabilization replay found no new defects, but the p95/benchmark-gate findings remain active.
Checked task rows remain supported by evidence.
